questions.question: Explain the difference between a one-dimensional array and a two-dimensional array.
questions.hint: A one-dimensional array stores elements in a single row or column, whereas a two-dimensional array stores elements in rows and columns.

questions.question: What is the syntax for declaring and initializing a two-dimensional array in a programming language?
questions.hint: The syntax for declaring and initializing a two-dimensional array is `int [row][column];` e.g., `int arr[2][3];`

questions.question: Write a C/C++ code snippet to declare and initialize a two-dimensional array and then access its elements.
